EasySaveDocs
Table of Contents

Interface ISaveStrategy

Namespace
ProjetEasySave.Model
Assembly
EasySaveWPF.dll

Defines the contract for backup strategies within the EasySave application. Implementations of this interface (such as Complete or Differential saves) must provide specific logic for copying files, handling states, and reacting to business software constraints.

public interface ISaveStrategy

Methods

doSave(string, string, List<string>, CancellationToken, ManualResetEventSlim, Action<int, string>)

Executes the core backup operation according to the specific rules of the implemented strategy.

bool doSave(string sourcePath, string destinationPath, List<string> priorityExt, CancellationToken token, ManualResetEventSlim pauseEvent, Action<int, string> progress)

Parameters

sourcePath string

The source directory path that contains the files to be backed up.

destinationPath string

The destination directory path where the backup will be stored.

priorityExt List<string>

A list of file extensions dictating which files should be transferred first.

token CancellationToken

A cancellation token used to safely abort the backup process.

pauseEvent ManualResetEventSlim

A manual reset event used to block or unblock the thread for pause/resume functionality.

progress Action<int, string>

An action delegate to report the overall completion percentage and the name of the file currently being processed.

Returns

bool

true if the backup completes successfully; otherwise, false.

getState()

Retrieves the current execution state of the backup strategy.

SaveTaskState getState()

Returns

SaveTaskState

The current SaveTaskState.

isBusinessSoftwareRunning()

Checks whether the user-configured business software is actively running on the host system.

bool isBusinessSoftwareRunning()

Returns

bool

true if an instance of the business software is running; otherwise, false.

setState(SaveTaskState)

Updates the current execution state of the backup task (e.g., RUNNING, PAUSED, COMPLETED) and notifies the parent task.

SaveTaskState setState(SaveTaskState state)

Parameters

state SaveTaskState

The new SaveTaskState to apply.

Returns

SaveTaskState

The newly applied state.

waitForBusinessSoftwareToClose()

Blocks the execution of the backup process and waits until all instances of the conflicting business software have been closed by the user.

void waitForBusinessSoftwareToClose()
